hero.lead: Your team collaborates here. Your agents work alongside them, each with its own persona, skills and memory. Conversations and memory stay on your machine.
hero.msg1Body: Can the agent draft the weekly report first?
hero.msg2Body: I'll have it read this week's meeting notes
hero.msg3Body: Draft's ready: 12 done this week, 2 blocked →
hero.artifactBody: 12 done this week · 2 blocked · 5 planned next week

agentAnatomy.eyebrow: Not one assistant — a team of colleagues
agentAnatomy.body1: Persona, skills, external tools, memory, workspace — five things, each isolated, never bleeding into one another. Skills installed on “Editor Assistant” are invisible to “Code Reviewer”; their memories are private too.
agentAnatomy.body2: You raise agents around specific jobs, instead of coaxing one do-everything assistant into doing nothing well.
agentAnatomy.sat5Value: Its own directory, never crosses over

conversation.eyebrow: How it actually gets things done
conversation.titleLine1: It breaks tasks down, asks when unsure,
conversation.titleAccent: and checks with you before hitting send
conversation.lead: It doesn't just hand you an answer. It breaks the task apart, pauses to ask when something's unclear, shows you the result, and waits for your nod before sending anything on your behalf.
conversation.userMsg: Turn this week's meeting notes into the weekly report and post it to #product-design
conversation.reply1Text: Sure — let me break this down first.
conversation.reply2Text: Before I write this up, one thing I want to confirm —
conversation.askQuestion: Two items are blocked this week — how should the report handle them?
conversation.askOpt1: List them under a separate “Blocked” section
conversation.reply3Text: Got it, I'll list them separately. Here it is:
conversation.artifactDoneItem1: Device authorization flow passed integration testing
conversation.artifactDoneItem2: Fixed unread count sync across devices
conversation.artifactBlockedItem1: Drive UI not wired up yet — backend is ready
conversation.artifactBlockedItem2: Model provider allowlist doesn't match the frontend options
conversation.reply4Text: Ready to send to #product-design — take a look at the wording, feel free to edit it directly:
conversation.confirmBody: This week's report is ready: 12 items done, including device authorization passing integration testing; 2 items blocked, see attachment for details.

team.titleAccent: and presence you can actually see
team.lead: Public channels, private channels, one-on-one DMs. Read and unread stay in sync across every device you use, and you can see who's online at a glance. The agent can read what's in a channel and draft replies for you.
team.msg1Body: Can the agent draft the weekly report first?
team.msg2Body: I'll have it read this week's meeting notes
team.msg3Body: Draft's ready: 12 done, 2 blocked this week. Attachment's in Drive.
team.msg4Body: Got it, I'll follow up on the blocked ones

mcp.body1: Same field names as Claude Desktop and Claude Code. Paste in your mcpServers block and it just works — stdio and http/sse are both supported.
mcp.body2: Each agent gets its own config, invisible to the others. Idle child processes get reclaimed after 30 minutes.
